concept_name: Electric Field due to a Point Charge (and its Field Lines)
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.1 Electric Charges and Fields (§1.6 Electric Field, §1.8 Electric Field Lines) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. HC Verma consulted for SEQUENCE (define E from force on a test charge → magnitude kQ/r² → direction by sign → field-line representation → density-means-strength → the two field-line rules), never copied. The atomic idea: a point charge fills the space around it with a field E = kQ/r² pointing radially (outward for +Q, inward for −Q), and the field-line picture — radial lines whose crowding encodes the field strength — is a map of that same 1/r² fact. Built for reviewer Asmi Singh (teaches this 2026-06-18); the field_3d renderer already ships point-charge scenarios, so this is assembly + a v2 JSON. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a).

real_world_anchor.primary: Run a plastic comb through dry hair, then hold it near tiny bits of torn paper on a table. The paper jumps up to the comb without being touched. The charged comb fills the space around it with an electric field, and that field reaches out and pulls the paper. The paper bits are little test charges revealing the field that was already there.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture a single charged raindrop high in a monsoon cloud. Around that one charge, the electric field points straight outward in every direction, getting weaker the farther you go — exactly E = kQ/r². Stack billions of them and you get the giant fields that end in a lightning strike.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: A Van de Graaff generator in a school physics lab piles charge onto a metal dome. Bring your hand near and your hair stands up along the field lines — each strand lines up with the radial electric field pointing away from the positively charged sphere.

r.name: Distance from the charge to the field point

formulas.field_definition: E = F / q  (force per unit positive test charge)
formulas.direction: radially OUTWARD from a positive charge, radially INWARD toward a negative charge
formulas.field_line_rules: field lines never cross; the tangent gives E direction; line density ∝ field strength

constraints: E is a property of space — it exists around the charge whether or not a test charge is present, and its value does not depend on the size of the test charge q used to probe it
For a single point charge the field is radial with spherical symmetry — magnitude depends ONLY on the distance r, not on direction
E ∝ 1/r² — halving the distance r quadruples the field strength E
Reversing the sign of Q reverses EVERY field vector (outward ↔ inward); the magnitude kQ/r² is unchanged
Field lines never cross (the field has exactly one direction at every point), and a field line shows the DIRECTION of the field, not the path a released charge travels along

aha_moment.statement: Crowded field lines mean a strong field — the picture IS the 1/r² law.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Watch the lines bunch tightly near the charge and fan apart far away; where they crowd, the field is strong — exactly where kQ/r² is large.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a single charge Q sitting in empty space. Bring a tiny positive test charge q near it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक single charge Q है जो empty space में बैठा है। इसके पास एक tiny positive test charge q लाओ।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ ఒక single charge Q ఉంది, అది empty space లో ఉంది. దానికి దగ్గరగా ఒక tiny positive test charge q తీసుకు రండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The test charge feels a force F — even though nothing is touching it. The space around the charge Q is doing the pushing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: test charge को एक force F feel होता है — चाहे उसे कोई touch भी नहीं कर रहा। charge Q के आसपास का space ही pushing कर रहा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: test charge కు ఒక force F అనుభవమవుతుంది — అది touch చేయకపోయినా కూడా. charge Q చుట్టూ ఉన్న space అది push చేస్తోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: We give that push a name: the electric field. The electric field E is the force per unit charge — E equals F divided by q.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हम उस push को एक नाम देते हैं: electric field। electric field E है force per unit charge — E equals F divided by q।
tts_sentences.text_te: మనం ఆ push కి ఒక పేరు పెడతాం: electric field. electric field E అనేది force per unit charge — E equals F divided by q.

tts_sentences.text_en: The field is a property of the space itself. It is there whether or not we put a test charge in to feel it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: field एक property है space की। वो वहाँ रहता है, चाहे हम test charge रखें या न रखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: field అనేది space యొక్క property. మనం test charge పెట్టినా పెట్టకపోయినా, అది అక్కడ ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick a point P at a distance r from the charge. The field there points straight out, along the line from the charge Q to P.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge से r दूरी पर एक point P चुनो। वहाँ का field सीधा बाहर point करता है, charge Q से P की line के साथ।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge నుండి r దూరంలో ఒక point P తీసుకోండి. అక్కడ field నేరుగా బయటకు point చేస్తుంది, charge Q నుండి P వరకు ఉన్న line వెంట.

tts_sentences.text_en: Its strength is E equals k Q over r squared. This is just the Coulomb force on a unit test charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसकी strength है E equals k Q over r squared। यह बस unit test charge पर Coulomb force है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని strength అంటే E equals k Q over r squared. ఇది unit test charge పై Coulomb force మాత్రమే.

tts_sentences.text_en: Move P closer and the arrow grows fast. Move it away and the arrow shrinks.
tts_sentences.text_hi: P को पास लाओ और arrow तेज़ी से बढ़ता है। दूर ले जाओ और arrow सिकुड़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: P ని దగ్గరగా తరలిస్తే arrow వేగంగా పెద్దదవుతుంది. దూరంగా తరలిస్తే arrow చిన్నదవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Halve the distance and the field becomes four times stronger — because of the r squared in the denominator.
tts_sentences.text_hi: distance को आधा करो और field चार गुना मज़बूत हो जाता है — denominator में r squared की वजह से।
tts_sentences.text_te: distance ని సగానికి తగ్గిస్తే field నాలుగు రెట్లు బలంగా అవుతుంది — denominator లో r squared ఉన్నందువల్ల.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now flip the same charge to negative. Something happens to every arrow at once.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब उसी charge को negative कर दो। हर एक arrow पर एक साथ कुछ होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు అదే charge ని negative చేయండి. ప్రతి ఒక్క arrow పై ఒకేసారి ఏదో జరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch. Every single arrow flips. For a negative charge the field points radially INWARD, toward the charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखो। हर एक arrow पलट गया। एक negative charge के लिए field radially INWARD point करता है, charge की तरफ।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి. ప్రతి ఒక్క arrow తిరిగిపోయింది. ఒక negative charge కోసం field radially INWARD గా, charge వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnitude is exactly the same, k Q over r squared. Only the direction reversed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnitude बिल्कुल same है, k Q over r squared। केवल direction उल्टा हो गया।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnitude సరిగ్గా అదే, k Q over r squared. కేవలం direction తిరిగిపోయింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The sign of the charge owns the direction: outward for positive, inward for negative.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge का sign ही direction तय करता है: positive के लिए outward, negative के लिए inward।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge యొక్క sign మాత్రమే direction నిర్ణయిస్తుంది: positive కోసం outward, negative కోసం inward.

misconception_watch.belief: The field always points away from the charge / a negative charge has no field
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Flip Q negative and watch every arrow reverse to point inward — the field is just as strong, only reversed.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Direction is set by the sign of Q: outward for +Q, inward for −Q. Magnitude kQ/r² is unchanged.

scene_composition.text: field lines = the map of E

tts_sentences.text_en: Instead of drawing one arrow at a time, we join them into continuous lines streaming out of the charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक-एक arrow खींचने की जगह, हम उन्हें charge से बाहर निकलने वाली continuous lines में जोड़ते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక్కొక్క arrow గీయడానికి బదులు, మనం వాటిని charge నుండి బయటకు వచ్చే continuous lines గా కలుపుతాం.

tts_sentences.text_en: These are the electric field lines — the map of the field in the whole space at once.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ये हैं electric field lines — पूरे space में एक साथ field का नक्शा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇవి electric field lines — మొత్తం space లో ఒకేసారి field యొక్క map.

tts_sentences.text_en: At any point, the tangent to the line gives the direction of the electric field E there.
tts_sentences.text_hi: किसी भी point पर, line की tangent वहाँ electric field E की direction देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏ point లోనైనా, line యొక్క tangent అక్కడ electric field E యొక్క direction ని చెప్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: For a positive charge the lines stream outward in every direction, like spokes from a hub in three dimensions.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक positive charge के लिए lines हर दिशा में outward stream करती हैं, जैसे three dimensions में एक hub से spokes।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక positive charge కోసం lines అన్ని దిశలలో outward గా stream అవుతాయి, three dimensions లో ఒక hub నుండి spokes లాగా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look closely at the spacing of the lines — it is not the same everywhere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: lines के spacing को ध्यान से देखो — यह हर जगह एक जैसा नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: lines యొక్క spacing ని జాగ్రత్తగా చూడండి — ఇది అన్ని చోట్ల ఒకేలా ఉండదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Near the charge the lines bunch tightly: the field is strong. Far away they fan apart: the field is weak.
tts_sentences.text_hi: charge के पास lines तंग bunch होती हैं: field strong है। दूर जाने पर वो fan apart होती हैं: field weak है।
tts_sentences.text_te: charge దగ్గర lines గట్టిగా bunch అవుతాయి: field strong గా ఉంటుంది. దూరంలో అవి fan apart అవుతాయి: field weak గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The crowding of the lines IS the field strength. Dense lines mean a strong field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: lines का crowding ही field strength है। Dense lines का मतलब है strong field।
tts_sentences.text_te: lines యొక్క crowding అనేది field strength. Dense lines అంటే strong field.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the picture and the formula are the same fact: the lines thin out as 1 over r squared, exactly like the electric field E.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो picture और formula एक ही fact हैं: lines 1 over r squared की तरह thin out होती हैं, बिल्कुल electric field E की तरह।
tts_sentences.text_te: అంటే picture మరియు formula ఒకే fact: lines 1 over r squared లాగా thin out అవుతాయి, సరిగ్గా electric field E లాగా.

misconception_watch.belief: Field lines are just decorative arrows pointing outward, all equal — spacing means nothing
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show the lines bunched near the charge and spread far away; the spacing carries the magnitude.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Line density encodes field strength — crowded lines = strong field, spread lines = weak field.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two rules people get wrong. First: can two field lines cross each other?
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो rules जो लोग गलत समझते हैं। पहला: क्या दो field lines एक-दूसरे को cross कर सकती हैं?
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు rules ని లోకులు తప్పుగా అర్థం చేసుకుంటారు. మొదటిది: రెండు field lines ఒకదానినొకటి cross చేయగలవా?

tts_sentences.text_en: No. If they crossed, the field would point two ways at one point — impossible. Lines never cross.
tts_sentences.text_hi: नहीं। अगर वो cross करतीं, तो field एक point पर दो तरफ point करता — असंभव। lines कभी cross नहीं करतीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: లేదు. అవి cross చేస్తే, field ఒక point లో రెండు వైపులా point చేస్తుంది — అసాధ్యం. lines ఎప్పుడూ cross చేయవు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Second: a field line is NOT the track a charge slides along. It shows the direction of the force at each point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दूसरा: एक field line वो track नहीं है जिस पर charge slide करता है। यह हर point पर force की direction दिखाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండవది: ఒక field line అనేది charge slide అయ్యే track కాదు. ఇది ప్రతి point వద్ద force యొక్క direction చూపుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A released charge follows the field's direction moment by moment — it does not ride the line like a rail.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक released charge moment by moment field की direction follow करता है — यह line पर rail की तरह ride नहीं करता।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక released charge moment by moment field యొక్క direction ని follow చేస్తుంది — అది line పై rail లాగా ride చేయదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Field lines can cross, and a charge travels along a field line like a track
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Show two lines crossing with a cross-out, and a charge sitting on a line with a cross-out, then the correct single-direction picture.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Lines never cross (one direction per point); a line is the direction of E, not the path of a moving charge.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive it. Change the charge Q, flip its sign, and slide the point P closer or farther.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तुम इसे drive करो। charge Q को बदलो, उसका sign flip करो, और point P को पास या दूर slide करो।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు దీన్ని drive చేయండి. charge Q ని మార్చండి, దాని sign ని flip చేయండి, మరియు point P ని దగ్గరగా లేదా దూరంగా slide చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the field arrow and the live readout. Double the charge Q and the electric field E doubles. Flip the sign and the arrow reverses.
tts_sentences.text_hi: field arrow और live readout देखो। charge Q को double करो और electric field E double हो जाता है। sign flip करो और arrow reverse हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: field arrow మరియు live readout చూడండి. charge Q ని double చేస్తే electric field E double అవుతుంది. sign ని flip చేస్తే arrow reverse అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Halve the distance and the field jumps four times stronger — feel the 1 over r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: distance को आधा करो और field चार गुना मज़बूत हो जाता है — 1 over r squared को feel करो।
tts_sentences.text_te: distance ని సగానికి తగ్గిస్తే field నాలుగు రెట్లు బలంగా పెరుగుతుంది — 1 over r squared ని feel చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same charge, same radial picture, one rule: E equals k Q over r squared.
tts_sentences.text_hi: same charge, same radial picture, एक rule: E equals k Q over r squared।
tts_sentences.text_te: same charge, same radial picture, ఒక rule: E equals k Q over r squared.

STATE_1.label: What is E — test charge feels a force

STATE_2.label: E of a point charge — one radial arrow

STATE_6.label: Two rules — never cross, not a path
